modules.name: Life
modules.description: Conway's Game of Life on a 24×24 grid (B3/S23 rule). Each tick, every cell updates based on its 8 Moore neighbours. Right-click presets include alternate cellular-automaton rules: HighLife (B36/S23), Day & Night (B3678/S34678), majority rule, voter model. Outputs total live count, generation count, oscillation/blinker gate, and polyphonic per-row activity.

modules.name: Schelling
modules.description: Schelling segregation model on a 24×24 grid. Two populations (red, blue) plus empty cells. Each agent is content if a fraction ≥ θ of its 8 neighbours are same-type; unhappy agents relocate to a random empty cell. Demonstrates that mild same-type preference (θ = 0.30) produces dramatic large-scale segregation. Outputs segregation index, unhappy fraction, average local same-type fraction, and a 'segregated' gate.

modules.name: Turing
modules.description: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion on a 48×48 grid. Two chemicals u and v diffuse and react; u + 2v → 3v (autocatalysis), v → P (decay). Feed rate F and kill rate k control the steady state. Different (F, k) produce spots, stripes, labyrinth, or solitons — Turing's mechanism for biological pattern formation. Outputs mean u, mean v, pattern variance, and an oscillation gate.
